conservation_statuses.description: Open Access: not in National Sensitive Species List

conservation_statuses.authority: Livro Vermelho dos Vertebrados de Portugal

conservation_statuses.description: Significantly Rare: Any species which has not been listed by the N.C. Wildlife Resources Commission as an Endangered, Threatened, or Special Concern species, but which exists in the state (or recently occurred in the state) in small numbers and has been determined by the N.C. Natural Heritage Program to need monitoring.

Widespread damming of rivers has destroyed the main nesting sites of this species, causing its numbers to fall. The number of breeding pairs is estimated between 2,300 and 3,000. Targeted measures carried out in recent years have brought the numbers back up, but remains vulnerable.

conservation_statuses.authority: Israel Nature and Parks Authority Red List
Ministerio de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sustentable y Aves Argentina - 2017

list.title: Oiseaux des Pays de la Loire

results.wikipedia_summary: The sand martin (Riparia riparia) or European sand martin, bank swallow in the Americas, and collared sand martin in the Indian Subcontinent, is a migratory passerine bird in the swallow family. It has a wide range in summer, embracing practically the whole of Europe and the Mediterranean countries, part of northern Asia and also North America. It winters in eastern and southern Africa, South America and the Indian Subcontinent.
